item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a terrible article/concept, but the Description really doesn't make it clear what the actual anomaly is. It takes a few paragraphs it looks like to even start talking about the voice, which is the actual SCP, it looks like. Downvoted for that, although it could be fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted, because the monologue is that good, in style and substance, but there are some places where it really needs work.
1) "a skin suit is equipped to…" I was not sure what a skin suit is, and the verb choice sounds to me like you're outfitting a character in an RPG game. OK, I lied, this is not something that REALLY needs work. The next ones are, though.

2) A blockage and a chamber beyond it are referenced, but they are not mentioned in the first place.

3) Yeah, there really needs to be a mention of what SCP-1202 is. Just like, "SCP-1202 is a phenomenon where affected subjects great a disembodied voice."

4) When an affected person "infects" someone else, do they stop hearing the voice?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… what is SCP-1202? Does the article actually, like… ever say what the SCP is?

Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just because I love the dialoge of the SCP, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it really elucid? All you need is to not come into contact with the current host for 1202, even then, wouldn't touching that person again just transfer scp-1202 back? It's not really a harm to foundation personell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of this story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The chamber behind the blockage was empty save for the frozen and emaciated remains of the body of one human male"
Where exactly was the chamber found? Under the ice formation in the lakebed, I'm assuming? Either way, mentioning that the chamber exists and where it was located is probably a good thing to do before describing its contents; personally, I did a bit of a double-take when I first read over that part of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Error: Bad Host

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would use those characters more in my SCPs and tales more if I was allowed but no that's not intentional.

It is allowed, but it's also a great way to earn downvotes if you don't do it well. Check out the FAQ.

To be honest, they're probably best avoided, but that's me. (Not anymore it's not.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would use those characters more in my SCPs and tales if I was allowed but no that's not intentional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I happened to notice the blacked-out doctor's name has the same amount of letters as "Jack Bright." Unless I counted wrong due to being tired.

Coincidence?

*goes back to lurking*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was based on the banana phone flash video.

item.title: Re: The one correction that would make this SCP readable...
item.content:encoded:
Huh, I guess not.
Looking here, it gets kind of ugly.

___This is ugly… Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Ut vitae urna vitae tortor tempor pharetra vel id dui. Etiam blandit nunc vitae lectus mattis sit amet consectetur elit iaculis. Praesent neque dui, condimentum ut accumsan id, vestibulum sed tellus. Pellentesque habitant morbi tristique senectus et netus et malesuada fames ac turpis egestas. Donec tellus massa, rutrum nec sollicitudin ac, ultrices ac sem. Cras ullamcorper, tortor vehicula facilisis porttitor, lorem nisi laoreet risus, eget ultricies erat felis quis felis. Phasellus quis risus nisl, et ultricies nisi. Curabitur sagittis metus nec justo scelerisque sit amet vulputate sem mollis. Aenean vel erat est, at tempor lectus. Nunc felis sapien, aliquam a facilisis vel, pulvinar nec augue. Nam ac mi dui, et ultrices elit. Quisque dignissim eros eget nisl cursus at lacinia nisl sagittis. Quisque vitae ullamcorper nulla. Maecenas nulla sem, viverra vitae varius in, adipiscing eu tortor.

item.title: Re: The one correction that would make this SCP readable...
item.content:encoded:
Doesn't it capture the tab key?

Indented
Double-indented.

item.title: Re: The one correction that would make this SCP readable...
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure how to indent on wikidot. I'm all for someone going in and doing it if it's a nag.

item.title: Re: Object class?
item.content:encoded:
I'll clarify containment procedures a bit.

Would it still be Euclid if the 'carrier' was confined though?

item.title: Object class?
item.content:encoded:
Why is this categorized as "safe"?

Dr Disembodied tried to persuade a Foundation agent to kill another Foundation agent, and apparently has all kinds of horribly dangerous plans for [REDACTED], [REDACTED], and [REDACTED].

And we're keeping him contained in a D-class?

Any Foundation researcher should have enough horrible and anomalous knowledge to qualify as Euclid all by himself. What happens if Dr Disembodied starts telling it to the D-class?

item.title: The one correction that would make this SCP readable...
item.content:encoded:
Indent your paragraphs in the "story/report" section of the SCP. Nobody, (not even the faction known as Nobody.)
wants to suffer through 'blocks' of text. When you indent at the beginning of a paragraph, you are giving the reader's brain a place to begin reading. Indentation also implies that a written piece has an air of professionalism, and is expected to be read as a story; rather than a text message from a friend.

On the other hand, kudos for correct spelling and punctuation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're right about the containment, changed.

Not sure I should touch the description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a little off and on about the description, it does need a bit of a touch-up for easy flow, maybe starting off with "SCP-1202 is a phenomenon, affecting only one individual at a time and transmitted through direct skin contact. This phenomenon causes…" blah blah blah. Also, somehow my attention skipped a line while reading the containment and I thought SCP-1202 was a skin suit before more careful reading, but I think that's more my wandering eyeballs.

The fact that not only is 1202 (supposedly) a Foundation researcher that got knocked loose from the bounds of reality, but that he's an egotistical, petulant prick sealed the +1 for me, above critiques notwithstanding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "This skin suit" made me think of Silence of the Lambs instead of a full body covering. Not really relevant to the article, but if I wanted to share.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one. SCP-1202 sounds the way I would expect an egotistical researcher from the Foundation to talk, especially to someone he considers beneath him.

A couple of nitpicks. I would expect in the description, the report would say which hand was on the forehead and which was digging into his ear canal. Was the other ear damaged as well?

Also, in the containment procedure, the first two sentences are a little confusing. It is hard to tell who should be fitted in full body clothing from the first sentences. Maybe something like "The subject exposed to SCP-1202 is to be quarantined from other site staff and personnel unless the subject is outfitted in clothing completely covering their entire body. This skin suit is to be equipped to the exposed subject immediately upon transfer of SCP-1202 to the subject." Just a suggestion-this SCP is already highly thought of. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think it's the Founder.

But it's some Foundation employee. Or it was, anyway.

I rather like that Dr. ██████'s guess as to how far back in time he went… is wrong. He doesn't have any magical knowledge, he just made an estimate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this intentionally implying that the Founder of the SCP Foundation became a disembodied voice and went back in time? Either way, I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The agent who first made physical contact with the corpse began to hear a voice that did not cease until he had made contact with another human. When this happened, he could no longer hear the voice.

I make a comment upon this section of words. When I do this, a new post was made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't sure if it would be tasteful or not to leave out "perverted doctor". Not important, but yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Will do, but that will take some thinking on my part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You should edit the description to make the description of the object be the first part of the description. It is a description. Take the recovery/discovery log and make it either an addendum or a second paragraph (I'd go with the second.)

That said, I love the addendum, and I'm upvoting.
